concepts: negative-funding pullback continuation after failed breakdown

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Recent journal flow is still explore-heavy enough to preserve the soft 70/30 balance, but the recent batches also show repeated low-trade failures on over-confirmed structures. Stay inside the supported universe and rotate away from the crowded ETH/BABY 1h lane and from BTC rejection logic. Target TAO 4h and demand 3 materially different specs that are not classic reclaim spam and not ETH-style channel shorts: one branch should test failed-breakdown re-acceptance into trend continuation, one should test a cleaner trend-exhaustion trap where downside expansion fails and price reclaims a medium anchor before continuation, and one should test a state-flip expansion branch that only arms after value is reclaimed and directional strength turns up. The goal is open exploration of TAO 4h with enough structural looseness to actually trade, while avoiding another ceremonial zero-trade continuation batch.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 15, and max_drawdown_pct <= 10.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.00, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: recent history still satisfies the explore-heavy doctrine, recent concentration is strongest in ETH/BABY 1h work, and TAO/4h is a supported rotation that changes both asset family and structure without locking back into a passing-family exploit loop.
